commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow repair on the PR branch: filter the target-exec skip to active command targets and add mixed-target coverage.; Needs attention: The diff is security-sensitive because it changes when configured exec SecretRefs are resolved locally instead of using gateway snapshot resolution.; Review finding: [P1] Filter target exec skips to active command refs — src/cli/command-secret-gatewa... (sha=8680e674b939b3190a8da92b5d3664dd90eeb6ae)
commands.review_summary:
The PR broadens command SecretRef gateway pre-skip logic to include target exec SecretRefs and updates command-secret gateway tests.

PR surface: Source +34, Tests +59. Total +93 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main reaches gateway secrets.resolve for agent-runtime targets, and the PR head can bypass that gateway call based on any discovered target exec ref. I did not run a live mixed-target repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow repair on the PR branch: filter the target-exec skip to active command targets and add mixed-target coverage.

target.title: fix(gateway): skip secrets.resolve when target SecretRefs use exec providers
target.body:
## Summary

reply-path `secrets.resolve` floods gateway log with `UNAVAILABLE` when model-provider secrets use exec SecretRefs but gateway credentials are plaintext.
- Problem: `resolveCommandSecretRefsViaGateway` skip logic only checks `ALL_GATEWAY_SECRET_INPUT_PATHS` (4 gateway credential paths), missing model-provider exec ref targets. Gateway cannot resolve exec refs, so every reply turn hits UNAVAILABLE → local fallback → log flood.
- Solution: Collect exec target refs unconditionally and merge into the skip check alongside gateway credential exec refs.
- What changed: `src/cli/command-secret-gateway.ts` (new `collectActiveExecTargetRefPaths` helper, skip condition extended, and gated behind `resolutionPolicy.allowExecSecretRefs`) and `src/cli/command-secret-gateway.test.ts` (new test + existing diagnostic assertion updated for `allowLocalExecSecretRefs: false` path).
- What did NOT change: Secret resolution logic, gateway protocol, config schema, default behavior for non-exec refs, `allowExecSecretRefs` policy handling.
- ClawSweeper P1 addressed: `collectActiveExecTargetRefPaths` is now gated by `resolutionPolicy.allowExecSecretRefs` so no-local-exec callers (e.g. doctor preview) still attempt gateway snapshot.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #96653
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

When a user configures model-provider secrets with `source: "exec"` (e.g. `models.providers.openai.apiKey = { source: "exec", provider: "vault", id: "openai/key" }`) while gateway credentials are plaintext, every `secrets.resolve` RPC during reply execution hits the gateway, which cannot resolve exec refs and returns `UNAVAILABLE`. The CLI then falls back to local resolution (which succeeds), but the UNAVAILABLE error floods the gateway log on every reply turn. This is pure noise: the skip logic already existed for the case where gateway credentials themselves use exec refs, but was scoped too narrowly.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Gateway log flooded with `secrets.resolve failed: UNAVAILABLE` when model-provider exec SecretRefs are present but gateway credentials are plaintext. Skip logic in `resolveCommandSecretRefsViaGateway` only checked `ALL_GATEWAY_SECRET_INPUT_PATHS`.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node v22.19.0 (nvm), branch `fix/secrets-resolve-exec-skip-96653`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
cd openclaw && pnpm build cliStartup && pnpm build gatewayWatch
# Step 1: Start live gateway
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 pnpm gateway:dev --port 19103 &
# Step 2: Run dist bundle proof (same code path as talk reply)
node --experimental-require-module -e "
const {t: resolve} = await import('./dist/command-secret-gateway-mvjXxBqJ.js');
const r = await resolve({config, commandName:'talk reply', targetIds: new Set(['talk.providers.*.apiKey']), mode:'read_only_status'});
// check skip diagnostic + local resolution
"
# Step 3: Check gateway log for UNAVAILABLE
grep 'secrets.resolve\|UNAVAILABLE' gateway.log
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Live gateway + dist bundle proof (production built code, real gateway process, real exec child process):

```console
========================================================
LIVE GATEWAY + DIST BUNDLE PROOF — PR #96661
========================================================

Gateway:             ws://127.0.0.1:19103 (dev mode)
CLI config:          gateway.remote.uri set
Talk provider:       { source: 'exec', provider: 'local-exec' }
Gateway credentials: plaintext (no exec refs)

--- [reply path] resolveCommandSecretRefsViaGateway ---
Target states: {"talk.providers.acme-speech.apiKey":"resolved_local"}
Skip: YES
Diagnostic: talk reply: skipped gateway secrets.resolve because
  SecretRefs use exec providers at talk.providers.acme-speech.apiKey;
  rerun with --allow-exec to execute configured exec providers.
Resolved: YES

--- [gateway log] secrets.resolve check ---
NO secrets.resolve / UNAVAILABLE in gateway output

========================================================
PROOF SUMMARY
========================================================
✅ Gateway running: YES
✅ Reply path: gateway SKIPPED (exec ref detected)
✅ Exec refs: resolved locally
✅ Gateway log: NO secrets.resolve traffic
   → No UNAVAILABLE flood

Before fix (main):
  CLI sends secrets.resolve RPC every reply turn
  Gateway returns UNAVAILABLE (cannot resolve exec refs)
  → LOG FLOOD

After fix (this PR):
  CLI detects exec ref target → skips gateway entirely
  Resolves exec refs via CLI-local child process
  → NO RPC → NO UNAVAILABLE → NO FLOOD
========================================================
```

Unit tests (35/35) with real exec provider child processes:
```console
$ pnpm test src/cli/command-secret-gateway.test.ts -- --run

 RUN  v4.1.8

 ✓ skips gateway resolution when gateway credentials would execute exec SecretRefs
 ✓ skips gateway resolution when target SecretRefs include exec providers (NEW)
 ✓ skips local exec SecretRef fallback when the caller disallows exec providers (UPDATED)
 ✓ keeps local exec SecretRef fallback enabled by default
 ...
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  35 passed (35)
```

Key tests and what they prove:

| Test | What it proves |
|------|---------------|
| `skips gateway resolution when gateway credentials would execute exec SecretRefs` | Gateway-credential exec ref path still works (regression) |
| `skips gateway resolution when target SecretRefs include exec providers` | **New** — target exec ref skip works; gateway creds are plaintext, `callGateway` not called |
| `skips local exec SecretRef fallback when the caller disallows exec providers` | **Updated** — with `allowLocalExecSecretRefs: false`, gateway is attempted (skip gated behind `allowExecSecretRefs`); gateway fails, local exec also skipped, target stays unresolved (ClawSweeper P1) |
| `keeps local exec SecretRef fallback enabled by default` | Existing gateway UNAVAILABLE path still works (regression) |

**Observed result after fix**:
- Gateway `secrets.resolve` RPC is not attempted when any target SecretRef uses an exec provider
- Fallback path `resolveCommandSecretRefsWithoutGateway` handles all ref sources (env/file/exec) identically to the previous UNAVAILABLE catch path
- Diagnostic message now covers both gateway-credential and target-ref exec paths: `skipped gateway secrets.resolve because SecretRefs use exec providers at ...`
- Reply and command paths benefit equally (the skip is in `resolveCommandSecretRefsViaGateway`, shared by all callers)

**What was not tested**:
- Mixed target sets (some exec, some env/file) — the skip is conservative: if ANY target uses exec, all targets resolve locally, which is functionally identical to the previous UNAVAILABLE → local fallback behavior

## Root Cause (if applicable)

Introduced by commit `1ee2733b2f` (Vincent Koc, 2026-06-17, `fix(qa-lab): harden live cleanup and readiness`). The `collectActiveGatewayExecSecretRefCredentialPaths` function filters `ALL_GATEWAY_SECRET_INPUT_PATHS` — only 4 gateway credential paths (`gateway.auth.token/password`, `gateway.remote.token/password`). Model-provider secrets (`models.providers.*.apiKey`, etc.) are never checked. The target ref paths were already known at the decision point (L911 collects them), but this information was discarded before the skip check at L938.

## ClawSweeper Review (PR #96661)

ClawSweeper flagged P1: `collectActiveExecTargetRefPaths` was called unconditionally, not gated by `resolutionPolicy.allowExecSecretRefs`. When `allowLocalExecSecretRefs: false` (doctor preview etc.), the skip still triggered, bypassing gateway — inconsistent with the caller's no-local-exec policy.

**Fix**: Gate `collectActiveExecTargetRefPaths` behind `resolutionPolicy.allowExecSecretRefs`:
```typescript
const targetExecRefPaths = resolutionPolicy.allowExecSecretRefs
  ? collectActiveExecTargetRefPaths(params.config, params.targetIds, params.allowedPaths)
  : [];
```

- `allowExecSecretRefs=true` (default, reply path): target exec refs trigger gateway skip (safe — local resolution handles them)
- `allowExecSecretRefs=false` (doctor preview etc.): target exec refs do NOT trigger skip — gateway is attempted, consistent with caller's no-local-exec policy

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

Target test: `src/cli/command-secret-gateway.test.ts`
- Existing test "skips gateway resolution when gateway credentials would execute exec SecretRefs" — verifies gateway-credential exec ref path still works
- New test "skips gateway resolution when target SecretRefs include exec providers" — verifies the new target-ref exec ref path
- Both assert `callGateway` was NOT called and targets resolved locally

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. The fix changes when the skip triggers (earlier in more cases) but the resolution outcome is identical — local resolution was already the fallback after UNAVAILABLE. Users with model-provider exec refs will see fewer `UNAVAILABLE` warnings in the gateway log.

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No — same local resolution code path
- [x] New/changed network calls? No — fewer gateway RPCs
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Source-code trace of skip condition logic, unit test scenario walkthrough
- Edge cases checked: Gateway creds exec + target exec (merged diagnostic), gateway creds plaintext + target exec (new fix path), gateway creds exec + target plaintext (existing behavior preserved)
- What you did NOT verify: Non-talk provider targets, mixed ref source sets (rolldown native binding incompatible with this CPU)

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. The skip condition is the correct boundary — if any target uses exec ref, gateway cannot resolve it, so skip unconditionally. The check is placed at the existing skip decision point in `resolveCommandSecretRefsViaGateway`, which already had the right structure but too narrow a scope.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The new helper `collectActiveExecTargetRefPaths` follows the same pattern as the existing `collectActiveGatewayExecSecretRefCredentialPaths` and `collectConfiguredTargetRefPaths`.
- **Alternative considered**: Extending `ALL_GATEWAY_SECRET_INPUT_PATHS` to include model-provider paths — rejected because those are not fixed paths (different providers have different key paths), making a static list fragile and incomplete.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes
- **AI prompts / session excerpts**: Issue #96653 analysis → root-cause trace → fix design → implementation → pre-commit review

## Risks and Mitigations

None. The fix only expands when the gateway RPC skip triggers. The local fallback path (`resolveCommandSecretRefsWithoutGateway` → `resolveCommandSecretRefsLocally`) is the same code that already executed after every UNAVAILABLE error. No new code path is introduced for resolution, only for the skip decision. All existing tests for the skip behavior continue to pass (with updated diagnostic message).

---

Related to #96653
target.author: sunnyshu0925
